findings.title: quic/aimet
findings.description: AIMET is a library that provides advanced quantization and compression techniques for trained neural network models.

findings.title: he-y/Awesome-Pruning
findings.description: A curated list of neural network pruning resources.

findings.title: 666DZY666/micronet
findings.description: micronet, a model compression and deploy lib. compression: 1、quantization: quantization-aware-training(QAT), High-Bit(>2b)(DoReFa/Quantization and Training of Neural Networks for Efficient Integer-Arithmetic-Only Inference)、Low-Bit(≤2b)/Ternary and Binary(TWN/BNN/XNOR-Net); post-training-quantization(PTQ), 8-bit(tensorrt); 2、 pruning: normal、regular and group convolutional channel pruning; 3、 group convolution structure; 4、batch-normalization fuse for quantization. deploy: tensorrt, fp32/fp16/int8(ptq-calibration)、op-adapt(upsample)、dynamic_shape

findings.title: neuralmagic/sparseml
findings.description: Libraries for applying sparsification recipes to neural networks with a few lines of code, enabling faster and smaller models

findings.title: openvinotoolkit/nncf
findings.description: Neural Network Compression Framework for enhanced OpenVINO™ inference

findings.title: ethanhe42/channel-pruning
findings.description: Channel Pruning for Accelerating Very Deep Neural Networks (ICCV'17)

findings.title: jacobgil/pytorch-pruning
findings.description: PyTorch Implementation of [1611.06440] Pruning Convolutional Neural Networks for Resource Efficient Inference

findings.title: alibaba/TinyNeuralNetwork
findings.description: TinyNeuralNetwork is an efficient and easy-to-use deep learning model compression framework.

findings.title: he-y/filter-pruning-geometric-median
findings.description: Filter Pruning via Geometric Median for Deep Convolutional Neural Networks Acceleration (CVPR 2019 Oral)

findings.title: JJGO/shrinkbench
findings.description: PyTorch library to facilitate development and standardized evaluation of neural network pruning methods.

findings.title: mightydeveloper/Deep-Compression-PyTorch
findings.description: PyTorch implementation of 'Deep Compression: Compressing Deep Neural Networks with Pruning, Trained Quantization and Huffman Coding'  by Song Han, Huizi Mao, William J. Dally

findings.title: neuralmagic/sparsezoo
findings.description: Neural network model repository for highly sparse and sparse-quantized models with matching sparsification recipes

findings.title: he-y/soft-filter-pruning
findings.description: Soft Filter Pruning for Accelerating Deep Convolutional Neural Networks

findings.title: liuzechun/MetaPruning
findings.description: MetaPruning: Meta Learning for Automatic Neural Network Channel Pruning. In ICCV 2019.

findings.title: rahulvigneswaran/Lottery-Ticket-Hypothesis-in-Pytorch
findings.description: This repository contains a Pytorch implementation of the paper "The Lottery Ticket Hypothesis: Finding Sparse, Trainable Neural Networks" by Jonathan Frankle and Michael Carbin that can be easily adapted to any model/dataset.

findings.title: mehtadushy/SelecSLS-Pytorch
findings.description: Reference ImageNet implementation of SelecSLS CNN architecture proposed in the SIGGRAPH 2020 paper "XNect: Real-time Multi-Person 3D Motion Capture with a Single RGB Camera". The repository also includes code for pruning the model based on implicit sparsity emerging from adaptive gradient descent methods, as detailed in the CVPR 2019 paper "On implicit filter level sparsity in Convolutional Neural Networks".

findings.title: NVlabs/Taylor_pruning
findings.description: Pruning Neural Networks with Taylor criterion in Pytorch

findings.title: anonymous47823493/EagleEye
findings.description: (ECCV'2020 Oral)EagleEye: Fast Sub-net Evaluation for Efficient Neural Network Pruning

findings.title: yehuitang/Pruning
findings.description: Code for "Co-Evolutionary Compression for Unpaired Image Translation" (ICCV 2019), "SCOP: Scientific Control for Reliable Neural Network Pruning" (NeurIPS 2020) and “Manifold Regularized Dynamic Network Pruning” (CVPR 2021).

findings.title: shekkizh/neuralnetworks.thought-experiments
findings.description: Observations and notes to understand the workings of neural network models and other thought experiments using Tensorflow
